title: Docs: Fill missing architecture contracts
description:
## Goal
Unblock detailed implementation ticket-writing by documenting the missing architectural contracts referenced in `docs/index.md`.

## Scope
- Runtime adapter contract (framework-agnostic)
- Component registry schema
- Inspector UX rules/semantics
- Preview session protocol (app/component/variant)
- Iteration Deck integration sketch (conceptual)
- Project Graph schema + patch/update protocol
- v0 in-scope deliverables + first user journey
- Override model + persistence/serialization
- IPC/sequence diagrams for key flows

## Success Criteria
- Each scope item is documented (new page or `docs/index.md` section) and linked from `docs/index.md`.
- Contracts are specific enough to write implementation tickets without inventing semantics during coding.
- Definitions stay framework-agnostic; React can be used as the first concrete example where needed.

title: Docs: Runtime adapter contract
description:
## Context
`docs/index.md` calls out runtime adapters (instance identification, click-to-select, applying prop overrides) but doesn't define the required contract or boundaries.

## Work
- Define a framework-agnostic runtime adapter contract: required capabilities, responsibilities, and data shapes.
- Clarify boundaries vs component registries and the extension host.
- Include a React-first example mapping (as an example, not as the contract).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Documents required adapter responsibilities: instance registration/identity, selection signaling, applying/removing overrides, and lifecycle/teardown.
- Defines the minimal data shapes (stable IDs, type IDs, prop addressing) used across the boundary.
- Specifies where the contract lives (new doc page or section) and links it from `docs/index.md`.
close_reason: Docs written and linked from docs/index.md

title: Docs: Component registry schema
description:
## Context
`docs/index.md` references registries and editable prop metadata, but the schema isn't formally defined. This blocks Inspector behavior and extension interoperability.

## Work
- Define a formal component registry schema (TypeScript types and/or Zod schema) for component metadata.
- Cover prop editability (editable vs inspect-only), control types, defaults, constraints, labels, grouping, and stable type IDs.
- Document how module registries compose and how project config overrides layer on top.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Provides a canonical schema for component registry entries, including prop metadata fields needed by the Inspector.
- Includes examples for at least 2 component types and common control patterns (enum, boolean, string, slot/children, etc.).
- Describes composition/override order (module -> project overrides) and links the doc from `docs/index.md`.
close_reason: Docs written and linked from docs/index.md

title: Docs: Inspector UX rules and semantics
description:
## Context
Scaffa's guiding principle depends on consistent Inspector semantics across core + extensions, but the UX rules aren't written down.

## Work
- Define the Inspector's visual language and semantics: editable vs non-editable, inspect-only, and opaque values.
- Define how overrides are represented vs defaults (and how reset/clear works).
- Define escalation paths (e.g. "inspect source" now; "promote to code" later) and extension consistency rules.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Documents the semantics and UI affordances for: editability state, override state, and source/escape-hatch actions.
- Defines consistency constraints so extensions contribute inspector sections without inventing new meanings.
- Links to related contracts (registry schema + overrides model) and is linked from `docs/index.md`.
close_reason: Docs written and linked from docs/index.md

title: Docs: Preview session protocol
description:
## Context
`docs/index.md` establishes preview sessions as a foundational abstraction (app/component/future variant), but the protocol and lifecycle aren't precisely defined.

## Work
- Define preview session types and lifecycle (create/start/stop/dispose).
- Define selection, override, and refresh flows for a session.
- Clarify responsibilities across main/renderer/preload/extension host/runtime adapter.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Precisely defines session types (app, component, future variant) and required lifecycle events/state.
- Defines selection + override flows (who emits, who stores, who applies, how refresh/reconnect behaves).
- Document is linked from `docs/index.md` and references the runtime adapter + overrides model docs.
close_reason: Docs written and linked from docs/index.md

title: Docs: Iteration Deck integration sketch (conceptual)
description:
## Context
Iteration Deck is explicitly deferred for v0, but the architecture needs an alignment sketch so v0 contracts don't block variants/snapshots later.

## Work
- Write a conceptual integration sketch for Iteration Deck: variants, snapshots, comparisons.
- Show how it layers on top of instance editing, registries, and preview sessions.
- Call out any required v0 constraints/invariants to preserve compatibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Provides a high-level conceptual model (no implementation) for variants/snapshots/comparisons.
- Identifies which v0 contracts must be designed to support it (sessions, overrides, registries, graph).
- Document is linked from `docs/index.md` (either in Deferred section or adjacent architecture sections).
close_reason: Docs written and linked from docs/index.md

title: Docs: Define v0 in-scope deliverables + first user journey
description:
## Context
`docs/index.md` has architectural pillars and an explicit out-of-scope list, but it doesn't clearly define what *is* in scope for v0 or the end-to-end user journey.

## Work
- Add a v0 scope section: what's in, what's out, and success definition.
- Write the first critical user journey narrative (open workspace -> start preview -> select instance -> edit props -> see overrides -> persist/share results).
- Ensure it aligns with the existing pillars and Deferred list.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Documents v0 scope with clear deliverables (enough to drive ticket-writing).
- Includes at least one end-to-end user journey that exercises preview, selection, inspector editing, and overrides.
- Cross-references related contract docs and is linked from `docs/index.md`.
close_reason: Docs written and linked from docs/index.md

title: Docs: Project Graph schema + patch/update protocol
description:
## Context
`docs/index.md` names the Project Graph as canonical truth and `docs/scaffa_extension_api.md` references `GraphQuery`/`GraphPatch`, but the graph entity model and patch semantics aren't defined.

## Work
- Define the Project Graph entity schema: routes, component types, instances, and relationships.
- Define ID strategy (stable type IDs, stable instance IDs where possible) and versioning.
- Define patch/update protocol semantics (incremental updates, ordering, delivery guarantees) and example patches.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Documents the graph's core entities + relationships, including required fields for v0.
- Defines patch semantics (what a patch can express, minimal guarantees, and example payloads).
- Clarifies producer/consumer roles (adapters produce patches; extensions consume) and links from `docs/index.md`.
close_reason: Docs written and linked from docs/index.md

title: Docs: Override model (non-destructive) + persistence/serialization
description:
## Context
`docs/index.md` says edits are "non-destructive overrides" applied to preview sessions, but the data model, persistence strategy, and invariants aren't specified.

## Work
- Define the override data model: addressing (instance + prop path), values, and precedence (defaults vs overrides vs config).
- Define lifecycle operations: set/update, reset/clear, bulk clear, and conflict behavior when instances change.
- Define persistence/serialization strategy for v0 (ephemeral vs saved, file format/location if saved) and how overrides are diffable/transactional.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Documents the canonical override model and precedence rules.
- Specifies persistence/serialization strategy and how overrides remain inspectable/reversible.
- Linked from `docs/index.md` and referenced by Preview Session Protocol + Inspector UX docs.
close_reason: Docs written and linked from docs/index.md

title: Docs: IPC boundaries + key sequence diagrams
description:
## Context
The multi-process model is defined at a high level in `docs/index.md`, but the concrete cross-process flows (preview start, selection, overrides, graph patches) aren't captured as diagrams.

## Work
- Add 2–4 sequence diagrams covering the critical flows:
  - Start preview session (app/component)
  - Selection (click-to-select) flowing to Inspector
  - Apply override and see it reflected in preview
  - Graph patch propagation to consumers
- Clearly label process boundaries (main, preload, renderer, extension host, preview runtime) and responsibilities.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contains sequence diagrams with named participants and explicit message directions across process boundaries.
- Diagrams align with the Preview Session Protocol + Runtime Adapter + Project Graph docs.
- Linked from `docs/index.md` (or embedded there if that's preferred).
close_reason: Docs written and linked from docs/index.md

title: v0: First end-to-end user journey (preview → select → inspect → override → persist)
description:
## Goal
Ship the v0 end-to-end flow described in `docs/scaffa_v0_scope_and_user_journey.md`.

## Success Criteria (Demo)
- Open a workspace configured for Scaffa.
- Start an `app` preview session.
- Click-to-select a UI instance in the preview.
- Inspector shows props with canonical semantics (editable vs inspect-only vs opaque).
- Edit an approved prop to create a non-destructive override; preview updates immediately.
- Reset/clear overrides confidently.
- Persist overrides to a diffable file (`/.scaffa/overrides.v0.json`).

## Architectural Constraints
- Keep the Electron multi-process model aligned with `docs/index.md`.
- Keep IPC boundaries aligned with `docs/scaffa_ipc_boundaries_and_sequences.md`.
- Keep schema/contracts aligned with:
  - `docs/scaffa_preview_session_protocol.md`
  - `docs/scaffa_runtime_adapter_contract.md`
  - `docs/scaffa_component_registry_schema.md`
  - `docs/scaffa_override_model.md`
  - `docs/scaffa_project_graph_schema.md`

## Epics
- IPC + preload gateway
- Workspace + config loading
- Extension host + modules
- Preview sessions + runtime handshake
- React runtime adapter (first implementation)
- Overrides store + persistence
- Inspector UI v0
- Project graph v0

title: Core: IPC backbone + preload capability gateway (v0)
description:
## Goal
Provide a typed, minimal capability surface from renderer → preload → main, aligned with Scaffa's multi-process model.

## Context
- `docs/index.md` (Process Model)
- `docs/scaffa_ipc_boundaries_and_sequences.md`

## Scope
- Shared protocol types/schemas used across boundaries.
- `window.scaffa.*` APIs exposed via preload.
- Main IPC request/response handlers and event broadcast channels.
- Runtime payload validation at boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- Renderer does not import Electron APIs; all effects go through `window.scaffa`.
- IPC payloads are validated (fail fast with structured errors).
- Domain epics (preview/overrides/graph) have the primitives they need.

title: IPC: Add shared v0 protocol types + Zod schemas
description:
## Context
Multiple processes need to share canonical shapes for preview sessions, selection, overrides, and graph patches.

Related docs:
- `docs/scaffa_preview_session_protocol.md`
- `docs/scaffa_runtime_adapter_contract.md`
- `docs/scaffa_override_model.md`
- `docs/scaffa_project_graph_schema.md`

## Work
- Add shared TS types + Zod schemas for v0 protocol payloads.
- Ensure main/preload/renderer/extension-host import from the shared module (no drift).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shared types are the single source of truth for cross-boundary payload shapes.
- Invalid payloads are detectable via Zod parsing.

title: Preload: Expose typed window.scaffa APIs for preview/selection/overrides/graph
description:
## Context
Renderer must not import Electron/Node APIs; it uses preload-exposed capabilities.

## Work
- Expand `src/preload/preload.ts` to expose typed APIs aligned with docs:
  - preview: start/stop sessions + subscribe to session lifecycle
  - selection: subscribe to selection changes
  - overrides: set/clear + subscribe to override state changes
  - graph: get snapshot + subscribe to patches

## Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer uses `window.scaffa.*` for all effects.
- API surface is minimal and typed.

title: Main: Implement IPC handlers + event broadcast plumbing for v0 domains
description:
## Work
- Add `ipcMain.handle` endpoints in `src/main/*` backing the preload APIs.
- Implement event broadcast to renderer for:
  - preview session lifecycle
  - selection changes
  - override state changes
  - graph patches

## Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer can round-trip basic calls (start session, set override, request graph snapshot).
- Events reach renderer reliably.

title: IPC: Validate inbound/outbound payloads with Zod + structured errors
description:
## Work
- Parse all IPC requests/events using shared Zod schemas.
- Standardize error return shapes for renderer consumption.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bad payloads fail fast with actionable errors (no silent no-ops).

title: Core: Workspace + scaffa.config.ts loading (v0)
description:
## Goal
Load a project workspace and its `scaffa.config.ts`, then expose effective configuration (modules, registry overrides, policies) to core services.

## Context
- `docs/scaffa_project_configuration_scaffa_config.md`
- `docs/index.md` (Explicit Editability, Modular + Extensible)

## Success Criteria
- Scaffa can select/open a workspace folder and remember it.
- Scaffa loads and validates `scaffa.config.ts` for that workspace.
- The effective component registry (module registries + project overrides) is available to the Inspector.

title: Workspace: Add open/select workspace folder flow
description:
## Work
- Implement workspace selection (folder chooser) and persistence of last-opened workspace.
- Surface current workspace path in the UI.

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can select a workspace folder.
- Restarting Scaffa restores the last workspace.

title: Config: Implement scaffa.config.ts schema + loader
description:
## Context
`scaffa.config.ts` is code (not JSON). It declares enabled modules and project-level overrides.

Related doc: `docs/scaffa_project_configuration_scaffa_config.md`

## Work
- Define the config schema (TS + Zod).
- Implement loading/validation for a workspace's `/scaffa.config.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invalid config fails with a clear error message.
- Loaded config is available to main + extension host.

title: Registry: Compose module registries + project overrides into effective registry
description:
## Context
Inspector behavior depends on the effective component registry.

Related doc: `docs/scaffa_component_registry_schema.md`

## Work
- Compose module-provided registries in module list order.
- Apply project overrides last.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Composition rules match the doc (later modules win; project overrides last).
- Disabled components/props are enforced.

title: Core: Extension host process + module contributions (v0)
description:
## Goal
Run trusted modules in a dedicated Extension Host process and plumb their contributions into core.

## Context
- `docs/index.md` (Extension Host Architecture)
- `docs/scaffa_extension_api.md`

## Scope
- Spawn/supervise a separate extension host process.
- Minimal activation model (`activate(ctx)`).
- Contribution pipelines:
  - component registries
  - graph producers (snapshot/patch)
  - runtime adapter bundle metadata (v0 stub)

## Success Criteria
- Extension host runs in its own process.
- A sample module can activate and contribute at least one registry + one graph patch.

title: Extension host: Spawn/supervise dedicated process from main
description:
## Work
- Implement a dedicated extension host process (separate from main/renderer).
- Add basic supervision (restart on crash, clean shutdown).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Extension host runs in its own PID.
- Main can detect and report extension host lifecycle.

title: Extension API: Minimal activate(ctx) + module loading from config
description:
## Context
Modules should never import Scaffa internals; they use the extension API.

Related doc: `docs/scaffa_extension_api.md`

## Work
- Implement a minimal `ExtensionContext` and activation pipeline.
- Load enabled modules from `scaffa.config.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A demo module can activate and register contributions.

title: Extension host → main: Registry contribution pipeline
description:
## Work
- Allow modules to contribute component registries.
- Deliver registries to main for composition and renderer consumption.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Registry contributions arrive deterministically and are visible to the Inspector.

title: Extension host → main: Graph snapshot/patch producer pipeline (stub)
description:
## Work
- Implement a channel for producers to send graph snapshots and ordered patches to main.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Main receives and applies at least one sample patch from extension host.

title: Core: Preview sessions (app) + runtime handshake (v0)
description:
## Goal
Implement `app` preview sessions owned by main, with lifecycle events and a runtime adapter handshake.

## Context
- `docs/scaffa_preview_session_protocol.md`
- `docs/scaffa_runtime_adapter_contract.md`
- `docs/scaffa_ipc_boundaries_and_sequences.md`

## Success Criteria
- Renderer can start/stop an `app` session via preload APIs.
- Main owns the session WebContents and emits lifecycle events.
- Runtime adapter handshake works (`runtime.ready` → `host.init`).
- Selection events are session-scoped and routed to renderer.

title: Preview: Implement app session manager (state machine + IPC)
description:
## Context
Main owns session lifetimes; renderer requests start/stop.

Related doc: `docs/scaffa_preview_session_protocol.md`

## Work
- Implement session creation/loading/stopping state machine in main.
- Expose `preview.startSession` / `preview.stopSession` via IPC.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Starting a session yields a sessionId and lifecycle events.

title: Preview: Create preview runtime WebContents (window/view) and load URL
description:
## Work
- Create the underlying WebContents for an app preview session (BrowserView or BrowserWindow).
- Load the configured URL.

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can see the preview runtime and interact with it.

title: Preview transport: Bridge runtime adapter events to main (runtime.ready, selection)
description:
## Work
- Implement a host↔runtime transport for the runtime adapter.
- Route `runtime.ready` and `runtime.selectionChanged` to main.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Main receives `runtime.ready` and selection events with correct session attribution.

title: Preview handshake: On runtime.ready, send host.init with initial overrides
description:
## Work
- On `runtime.ready`, main sends `host.init {sessionId, initialOverrides}`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Overrides replay after reload/reconnect.

title: Adapter: React runtime adapter v0 (selection + overrides)
description:
## Goal
Deliver the first concrete runtime adapter implementation for React, satisfying the framework-agnostic contract.

## Context
- `docs/scaffa_runtime_adapter_contract.md`
- `docs/scaffa_preview_session_protocol.md`

## Success Criteria
- Adapter emits `runtime.ready`.
- Adapter emits `runtime.selectionChanged` from click-to-select.
- Adapter applies `OverrideOp[]` (set/clear/clearInstance/clearAll) non-destructively.
- A demo harness exists to validate the flow end-to-end.

title: Runtime adapter (React): Implement adapter bundle skeleton + host command handling
description:
## Work
- Implement the runtime-side adapter bundle:
  - emit `runtime.ready`
  - accept `host.init` and `host.applyOverrides`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter can connect to host transport and exchange messages.

title: Runtime adapter (React): Click-to-select emitting InstanceDescriptor
description:
## Work
- Implement click-to-select.
- Resolve click to `{ instanceId, componentTypeId }` using a stable hit-test strategy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking selectable UI emits `runtime.selectionChanged` immediately.

title: Runtime adapter (React): Apply/clear OverrideOp[] non-destructively
description:
## Work
- Maintain an override map keyed by instanceId + propPath.
- Apply `set/clear/clearInstance/clearAll` ops.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Preview re-renders with the effective override value.

title: Runtime adapter (React): Add minimal demo harness app to validate flow
description:
## Work
- Add a minimal React preview target that Scaffa can load as an `app` session.
- Instrument at least 2 component types with stable instance identity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- End-to-end: start preview → click-to-select → edit prop → preview updates.

title: Core: Override store + persistence (v0)
description:
## Goal
Implement the authoritative override store and persistence model that powers Inspector edits.

## Context
- `docs/scaffa_override_model.md`
- `docs/scaffa_preview_session_protocol.md`

## Success Criteria
- Host supports `set/clear/clearInstance/clearAll` transactions.
- Overrides are persisted transactionally to `/.scaffa/overrides.v0.json` with deterministic ordering.
- Orphaned overrides are never silently dropped and can be cleared.

title: Overrides: Implement authoritative host override store (set/clear/etc)
description:
## Context
Host is authoritative for override state.

Related doc: `docs/scaffa_override_model.md`

## Work
- Implement override operations and session-target scoping.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Store behavior matches doc semantics.

title: Overrides: Persist to /.scaffa/overrides.v0.json transactionally + deterministically
description:
## Work
- Read/write overrides file for the workspace.
- Use deterministic ordering and atomic writes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- File diffs are stable and reviewable.

title: Overrides: Track and surface orphaned overrides (never silently drop)
description:
## Work
- Track overrides that fail to apply (instance missing/unstable identity).
- Provide clear UI/commands to clear orphaned overrides.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Orphaned overrides are visible and clearable.

title: Overrides: Route override changes to runtime adapter + renderer
description:
## Work
- On mutation, persist overrides and forward patch ops to the active session runtime.
- Notify renderer so Inspector can reflect overridden state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Preview and Inspector stay in sync after each override op batch.

title: Feature: Inspector UI v0 (registry-driven, instance-first)
description:
## Goal
Implement the instance-first Inspector UI using the component registry schema and override semantics.

## Context
- `docs/scaffa_inspector_ux_semantics.md`
- `docs/scaffa_component_registry_schema.md`
- `docs/scaffa_override_model.md`

## Success Criteria
- Inspector renders canonical editability states (editable / inspect-only / opaque).
- Editable props render canonical controls and produce overrides.
- Overridden state + reset affordances match the semantics doc.
- Escape hatches exist (open/copy source location when available).

title: Inspector: Replace placeholder panel with selected instance descriptor + states
description:
## Work
- Render selected instance descriptor (sessionId/instanceId/componentTypeId) when available.
- Render a clear empty state when nothing is selected.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Selection changes update Inspector immediately.

title: Inspector: Render registry-driven prop list with editability semantics
description:
## Context
Inspector semantics are canonical.

Related doc: `docs/scaffa_inspector_ux_semantics.md`

## Work
- Use effective registry entries to render editable vs inspect-only vs opaque props.
- Support grouping/order.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inspector semantics match the doc.

title: Inspector: Implement control renderers + TanStack Form wiring
description:
## Work
- Implement control renderers for canonical control kinds (string/number/boolean/select/color/json).
- Wire edits via @tanstack/form.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Changing a value produces a validated draft and commit action.

title: Inspector: Wire edits to overrides (set/reset) and render overridden/orphaned states
description:
## Work
- On commit, call overrides.set.
- Add reset actions (clear / clearInstance / clearAll).
- Render overridden indicators and orphaned override affordances.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Override UX matches `docs/scaffa_inspector_ux_semantics.md`.

title: Core: Project Graph v0 (snapshot + patch propagation)
description:
## Goal
Implement the canonical Project Graph store + patch protocol plumbing across extension host → main → renderer.

## Context
- `docs/scaffa_project_graph_schema.md`
- `docs/scaffa_ipc_boundaries_and_sequences.md`

## Success Criteria
- Main can serve a graph snapshot and apply ordered patches.
- Renderer receives patches and updates its graph views.
- At least one producer emits sample route/type nodes + edges.

title: Project Graph: Implement main-side canonical graph store + patch apply
description:
## Context
Main is the broker/authority for the canonical graph.

Related doc: `docs/scaffa_project_graph_schema.md`

## Work
- Implement graph snapshot storage and ordered patch application.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Duplicate/out-of-order patches are handled per v0 guarantees.

title: Project Graph: Expose getSnapshot + patch subscription to renderer
description:
## Work
- Add preload + IPC plumbing for snapshot + patch subscription.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer can request snapshot and receive patches.

title: Project Graph: Replace placeholder UI with live snapshot/patch view
description:
## Work
- Update `src/renderer/components/ProjectGraphTable.tsx` to render live graph snapshot.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Graph view updates when patches arrive.

title: Project Graph: Extension host emits sample snapshot/patch (routes + components)
description:
## Work
- Emit a small sample graph from extension host to validate plumbing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer graph view shows sample nodes/edges.

title: Demo: v0 journey walkthrough checklist
description:
## Goal
Maintain a single checklist for validating the v0 "first user journey" demo end-to-end.

## Demo Steps
- Open a workspace configured for Scaffa.
- Start an `app` preview session.
- Click-to-select an instance in preview.
- Inspector shows instance + props with correct semantics.
- Edit an editable prop; preview updates; Inspector shows overridden state.
- Reset/clear the override; preview returns to baseline.
- Persist overrides; verify `/.scaffa/overrides.v0.json` is updated deterministically.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checklist stays in sync with `docs/scaffa_v0_scope_and_user_journey.md`.
- All steps can be executed without manual DB/file hacking.

title: Build fails: @tanstack/router-devtools RouterDevtools export mismatch
description:
## Context

Running the production build currently fails due to an invalid import from @tanstack/router-devtools.

## Repro

1. pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
2. pnpm build

## Error

Vite/Rollup reports:

- "RouterDevtools" is not exported by "@tanstack/router-devtools" (imported in src/renderer/views/AppShell.tsx).

## Notes

Likely fixes:
- Update import to the correct exported symbol for the installed version (e.g. TanStackRouterDevtools), OR
- Gate devtools behind a dev-only conditional/dynamic import so production build excludes it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- pnpm build succeeds
- Router devtools still function in dev mode
